test: expand authoritative cross-validation to 85 checks across 18 vendors (#63)

* test: expand authoritative cross-validation to 85 checks across 18 vendors

Add 45 new cross-validation checks against linux-steering-wheels and
simracing-hwdb data for vendors not previously covered:

- Heusinkveld: VID + 5 PIDs (Sprint, Ultimate, Handbrake V1/V2, Shifter)
- Asetek: VID + 4 wheelbases + 2 pedals (Invicta, Forte, La Prima, Tony Kanaan)
- Cammus: VID + 4 PIDs (C5, C12, CP5 Pedals, LC100 Pedals)
- VRS: VID + DirectForce Pro + Pedals
- Simucube: VID + 4 PIDs (SC1, SC2 Sport/Pro/Ultimate)
- AccuForce: VID + AccuForce Pro
- FFBeast: VID + Wheel
- PXN: VID + V10 + V12 + V12 Lite
- Logitech: MOMO, DFP, DFGT, G25, G923 PS/Xbox, G Pro Xbox
- Simagic: TB-RS Handbrake
- Thrustmaster: TMX

Sources: JacKeTUs/linux-steering-wheels, JacKeTUs/simracing-hwdb,
Linux kernel hid-ids.h, Kimplul/hid-tmff2, berarma/oversteer.

commit.message:
fix(deploy): add explicit DNS servers to caddy container

Ubuntu systemd-resolved uses 127.0.0.53 as a stub resolver which is
unreachable from inside Docker containers. Caddy needs DNS to reach
Let's Encrypt for ACME certificate provisioning.

commit.message:
fix: use pull_request_target for auto-merge workflow

The pull_request event gives a restricted GITHUB_TOKEN for fork PRs,
causing 'Resource not accessible by integration' when calling
enablePullRequestAutoMerge. pull_request_target runs in the base
repo context with full token permissions. This is safe because the
workflow only runs 'gh pr merge' and never checks out PR code.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Speed up beeps 1.5x and increase calibration to 40s

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf: cache de arquivos estáticos em RAM + defer no brasao_b64.js

Problema: todos os arquivos estáticos (HTML, JS, CSS, PNG) eram lidos
do disco pelo Flask a cada requisição, passando pelo interceptor do
OneDrive que adicionava latência em cada open() de arquivo.

Solução:
- server.py: _preload_static_files() carrega TODOS os arquivos para
  um dict em RAM no startup (~2000KB, ~50 arquivos)
- Rotas /, /static/<f>, /<f>: servem do _file_cache dict sem tocar
  o disco, com fallback para send_from_directory se não encontrar
- index.html, fornecimento.html: brasao_b64.js (436KB) agora com
  'defer' — não bloqueia o render da página enquanto carrega
- index.html: app.js também com 'defer' para download paralelo

Resultado:
- brasao_b64.js: 2000ms → 9ms (222x mais rápido)
- index.html: ~500ms+ → 88ms primeira, 7ms subsequentes
- Todos os estáticos: 3-11ms (antes: centenas de ms via OneDrive)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: Phase 5-6 — CloseToTray, single-instance focus, overlay hover/gradient

- SI1: Second instance finds and focuses existing WhisPaste window via FindWindowW
- CLOSE1: Add CloseToTray config option (default: true) with settings UI toggle
  - When false, closing the window quits the app entirely via systray.Quit()
  - When true, preserves existing minimize-to-tray behavior
- CLOSE2: NotifyBackground toggle disabled/grayed when CloseToTray is off
- OVL-HOVER: Button hover and press visual feedback with TrackMouseEvent
  - WM_MOUSEMOVE tracks which button is hovered (brighten +20)
  - WM_LBUTTONDOWN sets pressed state (brighten +40)
  - WM_MOUSELEAVE resets hover, WM_LBUTTONUP resets press
  - All state changes are mutex-protected
- OVL-GRAD: Pill background uses GDI+ LinearGradientBrush
  - Top: 0xCC122435 (lighter), Bottom: 0xCC070F19 (darker)
  - Replaces solid 0xCC0A1A29 fill

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: sun arc uses location timezone and shows night position

The sun arc now computes the current time in the viewed location's
timezone (via Open-Meteo's timezone field) instead of using the
device's local clock. This fixes incorrect sun positions when viewing
locations in different timezones.

The visual is redesigned with a full day/night elliptical path:
- Upper arc (golden) = daytime, sunrise to sunset
- Lower arc (blue-grey) = nighttime, sunset to sunrise
- Sun dot travels the complete 24-hour path, going below the
  horizon after sunset

Fixes #2

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Prevent co-expression OOM on large studies (#12005)

* Prevent co-expression OOM on large studies with external group by

The rankCorr() aggregate function maintains one accumulator state per
gene group (~20K simultaneous states), each holding all sample pairs.
For large studies like pancan_pdmr_2025 (6,273 samples × 20K genes),
this exceeds available memory and crashes ClickHouse.

Add SETTINGS max_bytes_before_external_group_by = 2000000000 to spill
aggregation state to disk when it exceeds 2GB. This prevents OOM while
adding minimal overhead for smaller studies (~1.2x slowdown vs 1x).

Tested across multiple study sizes:
- chol_tcga (37 samples): 0.8s → 0.8s (no change)
- brca_tcga (1,101 samples): 15s → 19s (1.2x)
- pancan_pdmr_2025 (6,273 samples): OOM → 85s (fixed)
- aml_target_gdc (2,614 samples, 39K genes): OOM → 71s (fixed)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix e2e tests for null-correlation genes in co-expression response

Update co-expression e2e tests to account for genes with null
spearmansCorrelation and pValue (uncorrelatable genes: constant
expression, too few samples, or low variance). These genes are now
included in the response with null values.

- Filter correlated vs null-correlation genes before threshold assertions
- Add assertions verifying null-correlation genes have null pValue
- Remove sort-order assertion (endpoint does not guarantee order)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update src/main/resources/mappers/clickhouse/coexpression/ClickhouseCoExpressionMapper.xml

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
v3.0.0 (#574)

* chore(): Update to v3.0.0

New Features
feat(attendance): add disable courses, pre-hydration loader, and sync improvements

Disable Courses
- Add per-semester course disable/enable toggle with reason tracking
- New useDisabledCourses hook backed by user_settings.disabled_courses JSONB column
- Refactor UserSettings from bare hook (.ts) to Context-based provider (.tsx)
- Exclude disabled courses from dashboard aggregate stats, chart, and active count
- Sort disabled courses between normal and "no data" courses on dashboard
- Show "Disabled" badge on tracking and scores pages; sort disabled to end
- Display "Disabled" badge on calendar; exclude from absent-day dot indicator
- Course card: enable/disable dot toggle, reason selection dialog, reduced opacity
- Course card: separate official vs tracking-only attendance (purple panel)
- Add Supabase migration for disabled_courses column with JSONB default
- Clean up disabledCourses_ localStorage key on sign-out

Pre-hydration Loader
- Inject #prehyd-loader via inline script in layout.tsx (non-React DOM node)
- GlobalInit removes loader on React mount
- CSS auto-hide fallback after 12s if JS fails to mount

Sync & Normalization
- Cron sync: normalize EzyGo date keys (YYYY-MM-DD → YYYYMMDD)
- Cron sync: session ID fallback (session.session → numeric key < 20 → 1-based index)
- Normalize EzyGo API field typos (totel → total, persantage → percentage)

Fixes
- Suppress zero-change toast on dashboard, notifications, and tracking sync
- Narrow getCourseCode useCallback dependency to coursesData.courses
- Fix getEventStatus useCallback missing deps (isCourseDisabled, getCourseCodeById)
- ScoresClient: avoid in-place sort mutation during render

Docs & Tests
- Update copilot-instructions.md with disable courses, loader, and sync docs
- Update README.md with tracking/scores page disable effects
- Add/update tests for disabled courses, course card, calendar, scores, tracking
- Fix course-card.test.tsx TypeScript errors (mock typing, spread args)
- Update vitest.setup.ts with UserSettings mock

* Update .github/copilot-instructions.md

- Implement tests for TrackingClient to verify attendance status rendering and date normalization.
- Enhance route tests for cron sync to handle malformed authorization headers.
- Extend CourseCard tests to cover enabling and disabling courses, including bunk calculator visibility.
- Introduce ProtectedLoading tests to ensure loading component renders correctly.

commit.message:
Add runtime management APIs (#159)

* Add runtime management APIs

Add RuntimeService for listing simulator runtimes and downloading
platform runtimes via xcodebuild. Includes SimctlOutputParser for
parsing xcrun simctl JSON output (shared with SimulatorService).

Download pattern from ClientTools.Platform: xcodebuild -downloadPlatform.
Runtime listing reuses SimctlOutputParser.ParseRuntimes with filtering
by platform and availability.

Adds System.Text.Json dependency for netstandard2.0 target.

Closes #150

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review feedback: xcrun xcodebuild, version param, tests

- Use xcrun xcodebuild instead of resolving xcodebuild path inside Xcode bundle

commit.message:
Use Element.Id for stable element IDs across tree walks

Replace persistent ID maps with Element.Id-based ID generation:
- MAUI elements use Element.Id (Guid, stable for element lifetime)
- Duplicate AutomationIds use _{Element.Id[..8]} suffix (not positional)
- Synthetic markers derive stability from backing Element's Id
- No ReferenceEquals scanning; no stale cached state
- IDs rebuilt fresh each tree walk from element properties

Add platform EnsurePlatformStableId for non-Element objects:
- iOS/Mac: UIView.AccessibilityIdentifier
- Android: View.ContentDescription
- Windows: AutomationProperties.AutomationIdProperty
- GTK4: Widget.Name
- macOS: NSView.AccessibilityIdentifier

Add NavBarTitleMarker.Page for stable identity via Element.Id.
Add FindByIdRecursive for element lookup via tree re-walk.
Extract ResolveBackNavigation as shared helper.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor(monitoring): remove bare LLM calls from ops/ → agent tools

- error_clustering.py: stages 1+2 only (deterministic). Stage 3 semantic
  clustering now done by monitoring-ops agent via its LLM loop.
- semantic_matcher.py: exact + substring match only. LLM semantic muting
  delegated to monitoring-ops agent (it reasons naturally about mute lists).
- error_state.py: remove should_alert_async() — only sync should_alert() remains.
- auto_heal.py: update _cluster_and_filter() to use sync API.
- tools/monitoring_tools.py: 8 new SF tools for monitoring-ops agent:
  scan_incidents, cluster_incidents, get_error_status, should_alert,
  mark_alerted, create_heal_epic, add_mute, list_mutes.
- agents/tool_runner.py: register monitoring tools.

Architecture: background daemon (auto_heal) uses fast deterministic path.
Workflow (error-monitoring-cycle.yaml) uses monitoring-ops agent + tools
for full pipeline including LLM-based semantic reasoning.

commit.message:
Improve dotnet-pinvoke skill: stop signals, routing, inline anti-patterns (#72)

* Improve dotnet-pinvoke skill: add stop signals, When to Use, inline anti-patterns

- Reformat description to USE FOR/DO NOT USE FOR structured keywords
- Add 'When to Use This Skill' section with 6 trigger scenarios
- Add 'Stop Signals' section to prevent over-investigation on simple tasks
- Add inline anti-patterns near Steps 2, 4, and 5

Tested with multi-model eval (Sonnet 4, GPT-5.1, Haiku 4.5) — avg 4.8/5.
Assessment: https://gist.github.com/lewing/859ab56b0c37601804c03a5c601cfd8d
Training log: https://gist.github.com/lewing/e272e71dca0f4d1036a02a65796b5e1c

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use YAML block scalar for description to fix frontmatter parsing

Single-line description with 'USE FOR:' / 'DO NOT USE FOR:' colons breaks
YAML parsers that treat them as nested mappings. Switch to > block scalar.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: category disambiguation resolves group name from user input

Two issues fixed:
1. When user says 'gastos personales de Jose', the LLM now knows to
   pass category='Jose > Gastos personales' (prompt instruction added)
2. Even if the LLM passes just 'Gastos personales de Jose', the tool
   now checks if the input contains a group name that disambiguates
   between multiple exact matches, avoiding a redundant question

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
ballot-interpreter: remove contours timing mark strategy (#8058)

* ballot-interpreter: remove contours timing mark strategy

The "contours" algorithm for finding timing marks is no longer needed
in production; the "corners" algorithm is always used. Remove the
option entirely rather than keeping a dead code path.

- Delete timing_marks/contours/ module (fast.rs, slow.rs, mod.rs)
- Remove TimingMarkAlgorithm and Inference enums from interpret.rs
- Simplify BallotPage/BallotCard::find_timing_marks to always use corners
- Remove contours-related debug drawing functions from debug.rs
- Remove timing_mark_algorithm/infer_timing_marks from JS bridge and CLI bins
- Remove timingMarkAlgorithm and inferTimingMarks from TS API surface

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ballot-interpreter: fix rustfmt formatting

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ballot-interpreter: fix remaining contour references

- benches/main.rs: remove TimingMarkAlgorithm/Inference imports and the
  two Contours benchmark fixtures; remove timing_mark_algorithm field
  from InterpretFixture; update ScanInterpreter::new call to match the
  new signature (no algorithm parameter)
- debug.rs: correct stale doc comment on
  draw_candidate_timing_marks_debug_image_mut that incorrectly said
  "contour algorithm"

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor(ballot-interpreter): collapse `timing_marks::corners` into `timing_marks`

There's only the one algorithm now, so we don't need this extra level of
hierarchy.

commit.message:
Add feature modules: calculators, fleet, voice, inspection, home, settings

- feature/calculators: CalculatorViewModel (14 calculator types), CalculatorHubScreen grid, CalculatorScreen with input fields and results card
- feature/fleet: FleetViewModel (live Flow-based loading), FleetScreen with dashboard metrics and vehicle cards
- feature/voice: VoiceViewModel (TTS + network monitor), VoiceScreen with animated mic button, VoiceAssistantService foreground service
- feature/inspection: InspectionViewModel (AnalyzeImageUseCase), InspectionScreen with camera/gallery, FindingCard by severity
- app home: HomeScreen quick-action grid, HomeViewModel (vehicle count via VehicleRepository)
- app settings: SettingsScreen (privacy toggle, version), SettingsViewModel (PrivacyManager)
- data: CalculatorRepositoryImpl (Room + Moshi), CalculatorModule Hilt binding

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
